signature.instructions:
Translate Minecraft modpack/vanilla text entries from source_lang to target_lang.

=== Protected tokens (hard rule) ===
Source text may contain protected tokens: {{COLOR}}, {{RESET}}, {{ARG}},
{{BR}}, {{VAR}}, {{TAG}}, and numbered variants like {{COLOR1}}/{{COLOR2}}.
- The translation MUST contain every token exactly as many times as the
  source does — never invent, drop, duplicate, or alter a token.
- {{COLOR}} opens a color/format span; the following {{RESET}} closes it.
  After reordering words for the target language, the span must still
  wrap the same words it wrapped in the source. If the source opens a
  span without closing it, leave it unclosed — never add a closing
  token yourself.
- {{ARG}} is a runtime value slot (number/item/player name). Official
  Minecraft command messages frequently REORDER argument slots to match
  target-language grammar (e.g. English "{{ARG}} on {{ARG}} after scale
  factor of {{ARG}} is {{ARG}}" becomes, in zh_cn, "{{ARG2}}的{{ARG1}}在
  乘以倍率{{ARG3}}后的值是{{ARG4}}" — the 2nd English argument comes
  first). Reorder {{ARG}} tokens freely to match the grammar the target
  language actually uses officially — do not just keep source order by
  default. Preserve each token exactly (same bracket text), just move it.
- {{VAR}} and {{TAG}} are verbatim markup; keep them attached to the
  exact word/phrase they annotate.
- {{BR}} is a line break; preserve the line structure it implies.
- If the source string contains a garbled/malformed token sequence
  (stray braces, partial token text like "{{TAG}}RG}}"), infer the
  clean, correct token sequence that the real official string uses
  (tokens separated by single spaces where CJK punctuation doesn't
  apply, e.g. "-> {{ARG}} {{TAG}} {{ARG}}") — do not just copy the
  garbling verbatim into the "translation".
- In languages that don't use spaces between words (e.g. zh_cn/zh_tw/
  ja_jp/ko_kr), do NOT insert a space between a token and adjacent
  CJK text unless the official string does — Chinese/Japanese/Korean
  typesetting normally has no space around {{ARG}}/{{COLOR}}/etc.
  This includes places translators are tempted to add a space for
  readability (e.g. "最近的{{ARG}}位于{{ARG}}" not "最近的 {{ARG}} 位于 {{ARG}}",
  and "附魔费用：{{ARG}}" not "附魔费用: {{ARG}}" — also use the
  full-width colon ： not ": ").

=== Glossary ===
The glossary (when provided) is binding: whenever a glossary term
appears in the source, you MUST use its mapped target term verbatim.
Additional fixed terminology (zh_cn) unless glossary overrides:
- "advancement" → 进度 (NOT 成就) in command/UI strings.
- "Ravager" → 劫掠兽, "Silverfish" → 蠹虫 (not 银鱼), "Turtle Shell" (armor
  piece in equip subtitles) → 海龟壳, but "Iron armor" in the equip
  subtitle is officially "铁盔甲" (not 铁甲).
- "Spawn Egg" → 刷怪蛋 (NOT 生成蛋).
- "Armor Trim" / "... Armor Trim" (smithing template result name) →
  "...纹饰" (NOT "...装饰"). E.g. "Silence Armor Trim" → 幽静盔甲纹饰
  (note "Silence" here is officially rendered 幽静, not a literal
  "沉默").
- "Pottery Shard" → 纹样陶片 (NOT 陶器碎片). E.g. "Skull Pottery Shard"
  → 头颅纹样陶片 (note "Skull" → 头颅, not 头骨). "Blade Pottery Shard"
  → 利刃纹样陶片 (note "Blade" → 利刃, NOT 刀锋).
- "Sculk Shrieker" → 幽匿尖啸体 (NOT 尖叫者).
- "Pale Oak" (block series, e.g. "Pale Oak Hanging Sign") — "Pale" →
  苍白 (NOT 淡) in this series, e.g. "苍白橡木". Hanging signs follow
  the pattern "悬挂式+[Wood]+告示牌", e.g. "Pale Oak Hanging Sign" →
  悬挂式苍白橡木告示牌 (NOT "淡橡木悬挂标牌").
- Death message structure: keep these terse and use particles, not
  literal English structure. E.g. "{{ARG}} blew up" → "{{ARG}}爆炸了"
  (NOT "{{ARG}} 被炸毁" — no passive 被, no space, ends in 了).
- Banner pattern names (`block.minecraft.banner.*`) use compact
  heraldic-style formulas, NOT literal geometry translations. Learn
  the formula, not literal wording:
  - "[Color] [Charge]" patterns (e.g. skull/piglin snout) → "[颜色]+
    [noun]盾徽"-style or specific noun, e.g. "Black Skull Charge" →
    黑色头颅盾徽 (NOT 黑色骷髅纹饰).
  - "[Color] Base" (stripe_bottom) → "底+[颜色，常用单字]+横条", e.g.
    "Yellow Base" → 底黄横条 (NOT 黄色底纹).
  - "[Color] Base Sinister Canton" (square_bottom_right) → "左底+
    [颜色单字]+方", e.g. "Gray Base Sinister Canton" → 左底灰方 (NOT
    灰色右下方块). Note single-character color abbreviations (灰/黄/
    黑/紫/红 etc.) are often used in these compact banner names rather
    than the full "XX色" form.
  - "Bordure Indented" → 波纹边旗帜图案-style official term (NOT literal
    "边框凹陷纹饰" / "斜切/反转" wording). When uncertain of exact
    banner pattern term, prefer a short heraldry-flavored noun phrase
    over a literal geometric description, and check whether the
    official name uses "旗帜图案" as a suffix instead of the item's own
    name.
  - "Per Bend" / "Per Bend Sinister" (diagonal variants) → short terms
    like 左下三角/右下三角-style naming combined with color, e.g.
    "Purple Per Bend Sinister Inverted" → 紫色左下三角 (omit "反转"/
    "斜切" wording if the official term already encodes direction).
  - Keep all banner pattern translations concise: color + short
    pattern noun, never a verbose descriptive phrase.

=== Punctuation rules (zh_cn / zh_tw — CRITICAL, frequently missed) ===
- Use full-width punctuation throughout: full-width parentheses （）
  NOT ASCII ( ), full-width exclamation/question marks ！ ？ where the
  official string uses them, full-width colon ： in subtitle patterns
  and in any "Label: value" style string (e.g. container.repair.cost
  → "附魔费用：{{ARG}}").
- Ellipsis is a single full-width character "…" — NEVER three ASCII
  dots "...". E.g. "Feedback..." → "反馈…" (not 反馈...).
- Quoted text/parameter names use curly quotes “” NOT straight quotes
  '' or "". E.g. "Unknown criterion '{{ARG}}'" → 未知的准则“{{ARG}}”
  (not 未知标准 '{{ARG}}').
- Do not leave a space between "！"/"（" and adjacent CJK text or
  tokens unless official does so — check examples like
  '上传失败！（{{ARG}}）' (no space before the parenthesis), and do not
  add spaces around {{ARG}} inside CJK sentences (e.g. "最近的{{ARG}}
  在{{ARG}}（距离{{ARG}}格）" not "最近的 {{ARG}} 在 {{ARG}}（距离 {{ARG}} 块）").

=== Output format ===
Return the same keys as the input dict; translate values only. Never
leave a value untranslated unless it is a proper noun the glossary
says to keep as-is. Never output a value that is byte-identical to
a garbled/corrupted source string — always normalize to the correct
clean translation.

=== CRITICAL: match official Minecraft localization, don't paraphrase ===
Most entries come directly from vanilla Minecraft (translation keys
like `subtitles.*`, `block.minecraft.banner.*`, `options.*`,
`optimizeWorld.*`, `narrator.*`, `commands.*`, `advancements.*`,
`argument.*`, `item.minecraft.*`, `death.attack.*`, etc.). These
strings already have an official, shipped translation for every
target language. Your job is to RECALL that exact official wording,
not construct your own grammatically-correct-but-different rendering.
A fluent but differently-worded translation scores as WRONG even if
it's a perfectly reasonable translation. Concrete rules and examples:

1. SUBTITLE keys (`subtitles.entity.*`, `subtitles.block.*`,
   `subtitles.item.*`, `subtitles.event.*`) follow the pattern
   "Subject：Action" in zh_cn (full-width colon, no verb conjugation
   like 了/着), where BOTH subject and action must match the official
   short noun/word Mojang used — not a literal or descriptive
   translation of the English verb, and NOT a "XX声" or "…的一声"
   suffix pattern unless that IS the official word. The action is
   typically a terse 2-4 character noun, often not what a dictionary
   would suggest. Examples of confirmed official mappings (memorize
   the pattern, not just literal meaning):
   - 'Skeleton rattles' → '骷髅：咯咯声'
   - 'Ravager hurts' → '劫掠兽：受伤'
   - 'Silverfish hurts' → '蠹虫：受伤'
   - 'Parrot whirs' (imitating Breeze) → '鹦鹉：呼啸' (NOT 嗡嗡声)
   - 'Parrot gurgles' (imitating Drowned) → '鹦鹉：呻吟' (NOT 咕噜声)
   - 'Parrot murmurs' (imitating Evoker) → '鹦鹉：咕哝' (NOT 低语声)
   - 'Wolf whines' → '狼：呜咽' (NOT 呜呜声)
   - 'Zombie Villager dies' → '僵尸村民：死亡'
   - 'Iron armor clanks' (equip subtitle) → '铁盔甲：铿锵' (NOT 铁甲：叮当声)
   - 'Book thumps' (put down) → '书：放置' (describes the ACTION "placed",
     not the sound — NOT 书本：砰的一声)
   - `subtitles.event.*` keys ALSO use "Subject：Action" even though the
     English reads as a full sentence: 'Ominous trial looms nearby' →
     '不祥试炼：迫在眉睫' (NOT a full descriptive sentence like
     不详的试炼即将降临)
   When you don't know the exact official word, still pick a terse
   2-4 character noun/verb-noun in Mojang's idiom rather than a longer
   descriptive phrase, and avoid generic sound-effect suffixes like
   声/的一声 unless confident that's official.

2. COMMAND FEEDBACK / ARGUMENT ERROR / DEATH MESSAGE keys
   (`commands.*`, `argument.*`, `death.attack.*`) have official
   strings that often restructure the whole sentence with Chinese
   particles (的/在/后/是/时/将/把/应/以/了) and reorder {{ARG}} slots,
   and use curly quotes “” for quoted names — they are NOT a
   word-for-word translation of the English, and are usually SHORTER
   than a literal rendering. No spaces between CJK and tokens. Recall
   the actual shipped phrasing rather than translating literally.
   Examples:
   - 'Granted the advancement {{ARG}} to {{ARG}}' → '已将进度{{ARG}}赋予{{ARG}}'
     (advancement=进度, structure "已将X赋予Y", no spaces)
   - '{{ARG}} on {{ARG}} after scale factor of {{ARG}} is {{ARG}}' →
     '{{ARG2}}的{{ARG1}}在乘以倍率{{ARG3}}后的值是{{ARG4}}' (args reordered)
   - "Tracing can't be used with return run" → '使用/return run时无法追踪'
     (keep literal command syntax "/return run" untranslated, structure
     "使用X时无法Y")
   - "Expected closing ] for block state properties" → '方块属性应以]结束'
     (short, restructured — "block state properties"→方块属性,
     structure "X应以Y结束")
   - "Unknown criterion '{{ARG}}'" → '未知的准则“{{ARG}}”' (curly quotes,
     准则 not 标准)
   - "{{ARG}} blew up" → "{{ARG}}爆炸了" (no 被/passive, no space)
   - Enchantment/cost labels use full-width colon directly after the
     label with no space: "Enchantment Cost: {{ARG}}" → "附魔费用：{{ARG}}"
   Prefer compact, particle-driven official command-message grammar
   over literal English word order, and keep these translations SHORT
   — official command error/death strings are usually terse, not verbose.

3. ADVANCEMENT title/description keys (`advancements.*.title`,
   `advancements.*.description`) are often creative/idiomatic
   wordplay in official zh_cn, NOT literal translations of the
   English pun/phrase. E.g. "This Boat Has Legs" (advancement for
   riding a Strider) → '画船添足' (an idiom-style coinage playing on
   画蛇添足, NOT a literal '这艘船有腿'). When uncertain of the exact
   official coinage, prefer a short 4-character idiom-style or
   punchy phrase over a literal descriptive translation — but do not
   fabricate one confidently; use your best recollection of the
   actual shipped string.

4. Banner pattern keys — see the Glossary section above for concrete
   confirmed formulas (base/canton/charge/bend patterns). In general:
   official banner pattern names are SHORT (often 3-5 characters
   total: color abbreviation + direction/position + pattern noun),
   use heraldry-flavored nouns (盾徽, 横条, 三角, 方, etc.), and often
   do NOT spell out the color in full "XX色" form. Never produce a
   literal geometric description like "边框凹陷"/"右下方块"/"底纹" — these
   score very poorly against official terms.

5. ITEM/BLOCK name keys (`item.minecraft.*`, `block.minecraft.*`)
   must match the exact official noun choices, which are often NOT
   the literal dictionary translation of the English word (see
   glossary section above for confirmed examples: 刷怪蛋, 纹饰, 纹样陶片,
   幽静 for "Silence", 头颅 for "Skull", 利刃 for "Blade" in pottery
   shard names, 幽匿尖啸体 for "Sculk Shrieker", 苍白 for "Pale" in the
   Pale Oak block series). When uncertain, prefer the compact 2-4
   character official-style noun phrase Mojang typically uses for
   items, not a longer descriptive compound.

6. Numeric dimension/size strings use the multiplication sign "×"
   (not "x"), and any parenthetical descriptor should use the
   official adjective in full-width parentheses, not a literal
   translation:
   - '13x13 (Showoff)' → '13×13（显著）' (not 13x13（展示）or 13x13 (展示))

7. More generally, for ANY vanilla key, prioritize matching Mojang's
   established terminology/style over inventing new phrasing — correct
   entity names, correct block/item names, official particle-driven
   sentence structure, official punctuation (full-width brackets,
   single-character ellipsis "…", curly quotes “”), and the terse,
   idiomatic register Mojang uses for status/progress/subtitle/command/
   death messages. When genuinely uncertain of the exact official
   string, still mimic this compact, idiomatic register rather than
   producing a longer, literal, dictionary-style paraphrase —
   length/register should stay proportionate to the (typically very
   short) official original. A wrong-but-official-style guess is
   preferable to a fluent literal paraphrase.

=== Language-specific tone ===
- Korean (ko_kr): natural gamer-facing tone; never append the English
  original in parentheses/brackets; use established Korean item names
  instead of romanization.
- Japanese (ja_jp): plain polite register for UI text.
- Chinese (zh_cn / zh_tw): strictly follow official Minecraft
  terminology of the respective variant, as detailed above — official
  wording (including punctuation: full-width brackets, single-char
  ellipsis, curly quotes, full-width colon, no stray spaces around
  tokens) takes precedence over a "correct but different" translation.

=== General translation quality ===
- Keep translation length/register proportionate to the source; don't
  produce translations that are drastically shorter or longer than
  the expected official phrasing (this is a frequent, easily-checked
  failure mode — e.g. a 4-word English banner pattern name should not
  collapse into a 2-character Chinese phrase or expand into an
  8-character descriptive phrase when the official term is 3-5 chars).
- When unsure of the exact official string, still choose wording that
  mimics Mojang's typical concise, idiomatic style rather than a
  literal or descriptive paraphrase.
- Double-check punctuation style (full-width vs half-width, curly
  quotes vs straight quotes, single "…" vs "...", presence/absence of
  spaces around tokens) before finalizing every zh_cn/zh_tw/ja_jp/
  ko_kr answer — this is a common, easily avoidable source of
  low-similarity scoring even when wording is otherwise correct.

fields.description: mod name, content type (quest/item/tooltip/guidebook), file path hint
binding term rules in 'source = target' form; MUST be followed
key -> source text with protected {{KIND}} tokens
exactly the same keys -> translated text

signature.instructions:
Fix a translation that failed programmatic validation.

Address every listed error. Keep the parts of the translation that are
already correct. Every protected token from the source ({{COLOR}} opens
a color span, {{RESET}} closes it, {{ARG}} is a value slot) must appear
in the fix exactly as many times as in the source, copied exactly as
written. The glossary is binding.
